problem: What's the name of the women's liberal arts college in Cambridge, Massachusetts?

response.answer: The name of the women's liberal arts college in Cambridge, Massachusetts, is Radcliffe College.
search_results: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radcliffe_College: Radcliffe College was a women's liberal arts college in Cambridge, Massachusetts, that was founded in 1879. In 1999, it was fully incorporated into Harvard.

https://www.c-span.org/organization/radcliffe-college/26971/: Radcliffe College was a women's liberal arts college in Cambridge, Massachusetts, and functioned as a female coordinate institution for the all-male Harvard.

problem: In whose honor was the Leipzig 1877 tournament organized?

response.answer: The Leipzig 1877 tournament was organized in honor of Adolf Anderssen. It was named the "Anderssen-Feier" (Anderssen Celebration) to celebrate him.
search_results: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Adolf_Anderssen: The Leipzig 1877 tournament was organised in his honour and named the "Anderssen-Feier" (Anderssen Celebration); Anderssen finished second in the tournament.

https://www.chessgames.com/perl/chess.pl?tid=79429: The Leipzig 1877 event (organized by the Mitteldeutscher Schachbund) was called the Anderssen-Feier and held in honor of Adolf Anderssen. He seemed to have lost none of his strength, finishing =2nd in the tournament.
https://www.schachbund.de/die-anderssen-feier-in-leipzig-1877.html: Die Anderssen-Feier in Leipzig 1877 was held in honor of Adolf Anderssen.
https://kids.kiddle.co/Adolf_Anderssen: The Leipzig 1877 tournament was held to honor Anderssen. It was called the "Anderssen-Feier" (Anderssen Celebration).

https://de.chessbase.com/post/200-jahre-adolf-anderssen: Anlässlich seines 50-jährigen Schachjubiläums wurde am Abend des 18. Juli 1877, während der "Anderssen-Feier" in Leipzig der Deutsche Schachbund gegründet.

problem: According to Karl Küchler, what did Empress Elizabeth of Austria's favorite sculpture depict, which was made for her villa Achilleion at Corfu?

response.answer: According to Karl Küchler, Empress Elizabeth of Austria's favorite sculpture, which was made for her villa Achilleion at Corfu, depicted Achilles.
search_results: According to Karl Küchler, Empress Elizabeth of Austria's (Sisi's) favorite sculpture at her villa Achilleion at Corfu depicted Achilles.
